abstract: We evaluate thresholds for the surface code under biased noise models using realistic gate decompositions and circuit scheduling. We compare analytical bounds with Monte Carlo simulations and quantify logical error rates across code distances.
constraints: Use Qiskit Aer or Stim if available for simulation
Estimate logical error rates for code distances d in {3,5,7,9}
Sweep physical error rates p in [1e-4, 5e-2]
Compare simulation results against theoretical predictions and prior state-of-the-art results
full_description: Study the surface code under noise models with dephasing bias (Z-dominant) and gate-dependent error rates. Derive expected threshold behavior from literature and implement simulations to estimate logical error rates for distances d ∈ {3,5,7,9}. Use Qiskit Aer (or Stim if available) to build circuits for repeated syndrome extraction with lattice surgery style checks. For each bias ratio η ∈ {1, 5, 20, 50} and physical error p ∈ [1e-4, 5e-2], estimate logical error per round and fit threshold curves. Compare against theoretical predictions and prior SOTA results; document limitations and mismatches.
goals: Evaluate surface-code thresholds under biased noise models with realistic gate decompositions and circuit scheduling
Derive expected threshold behavior from literature
Implement Monte Carlo simulations for repeated syndrome extraction circuits
Estimate logical error per round for code distances d in {3,5,7,9}
Fit threshold curves across bias ratios eta in {1, 5, 20, 50} and physical error rates p in [1e-4, 5e-2]
Compare simulation outcomes with theoretical predictions and prior state-of-the-art results
Document limitations and mismatches between theory and simulation

constraints: Use Qiskit Aer or Stim if available for simulation
Estimate logical error rates for code distances d in {3,5,7,9}
Sweep physical error rates p in [1e-4, 5e-2]
Compare simulation results against theoretical predictions and prior state-of-the-art results

original_problem.request_text:
Title: Noise-Biased Surface Code Thresholds Under Realistic Gate Sets
Abstract: We evaluate thresholds for the surface code under biased noise models using realistic gate decompositions and circuit scheduling. We compare analytical bounds with Monte Carlo simulations and quantify logical error rates across code distances.
Full Description: Study the surface code under noise models with dephasing bias (Z-dominant) and gate-dependent error rates. Derive expected threshold behavior from literature and implement simulations to estimate logical error rates for distances d ∈ {3,5,7,9}. Use Qiskit Aer (or Stim if available) to build circuits for repeated syndrome extraction with lattice surgery style checks. For each bias ratio η ∈ {1, 5, 20, 50} and physical error p ∈ [1e-4, 5e-2], estimate logical error per round and fit threshold curves. Compare against theoretical predictions and prior SOTA results; document limitations and mismatches.
Problem Type: Open Question
Difficulty: Frontier
Domain: Quantum Information
Originator: Admin Curator
Compute Budget: TBD
Target Deadline: 2026-03-04

Categories: Quantum Information
